popularStory.description: John Clifford, 9th Baron Clifford, 9th Lord of Skipton (8 April 1435 – 28 March 1461) was a Lancastrian military leader during the Wars of the Roses in England. The Clifford family was one of the most …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford於1435年4月8日出生在英國越可爾康尼斯布盧 卡素時，他的父親Sir Thomas Clifford20歲，母親Joan Dacre21歲。他於大約1452年與Margaret Bromflete結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1461年3月29日在英國越可爾費利布利其享年25歲，埋葬在英國越可爾費利布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford was born on 8 April 1435, in Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas Clifford, was 20 and his mother, Joan Dacre, was 21. He married Margaret Bromflete about 1452. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 29 March 1461, in Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 25, and was buried in Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford est né le 8 avril 1435, à Conisbrough Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Clifford, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Joan Dacre, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Bromflete environ 1452. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 29 mars 1461, à Ferrybridge, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 25 ans, et a été enterré à Ferrybridge, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.Aughton, Yorkshire East Riding, England: Aughton, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford am 8. April 1435 in Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas Clifford 20 und seine Mutter Joan Dacre 21. Er hat um 1452 Margaret Bromflete geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 29. März 1461 im Alter von 25 Jahren in Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Bromflete circa 1452. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 marzo 1461, nel Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 25 anni, e fu sepolto a Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford(이)가 1435년 4월 8일 영국 요르크실르 코니스브루그h 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Clifford(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Dacre(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1452년 Margaret Bromflete(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1461년 3월 29일 영국 요르크실르 페리브리지에서 25세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 페리브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford nasceu em 8 de abril de 1435, em Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Clifford, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Joan Dacre, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Bromflete aproximadamente 1452. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de março de 1461, em Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 25 anos, e foi sepultado em Ferrybridge, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Clifford 9th Baron Clifford родился 8 апреля 1435 г. в местности конисброую кастл, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Clifford было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Dacre, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Bromflete около 1452 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 29 марта 1461 г. в местности фиррибридж, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 25 лет и был похоронен в местности фиррибридж, еркшир, Англия.
